query: Ethereum Bitcoin relative strength factors June 2026, ETH ETF flows, BTC dominance trend
answer:
Excellent — I now have substantial data from two research agents. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

---

# Ethereum / Bitcoin Relative Strength Factors — June 2026

## 1. Current Prices & ETH/BTC Ratio (as of June 2, 2026)

| Asset | Price (USD) | 24h Change |
|-------|------------|------------|
| **BTC** | **$70,403** | -3.97% |
| **ETH** | **$1,987** | -0.33% |
| **ETH/BTC** | **0.02822** | +3.7% (daily bounce) |

The ETH/BTC ratio stands at **0.02822**, near its 2026 low of **0.0272** set on May 28. This ratio has been in a persistent **9-month downtrend** since peaking at **0.04213** in August 2025. The June 2 bounce (+3.7%) — with ETH falling less than BTC — is the most notable single-day recovery in weeks.

**Key ETH/BTC reference points:**
- 1-year high: **0.04213** (Aug 25, 2025)
- 1-year low: **0.02209** (Jun 23, 2025)
- 2026 low: **0.02720** (May 28, 2026)
- 14-day RSI: **65.6** (neutral-to-slightly bullish, recovering from oversold)

## 2. Multi-Timeframe Relative Performance

| Timeframe | BTC | ETH | Relative Winner |
|-----------|-----|-----|-----------------|
| **24h** | -3.97% | -0.33% | **ETH** |
| **7d** | -8.19% | -5.06% | **ETH** |
| **14d** | -8.39% | -6.83% | **ETH** |
| **30d** | -9.96% | -13.67% | BTC |
| **60d** | +5.72% | -3.29% | BTC |
| **200d** | -28.91% | -38.29% | BTC |
| **1y** | -33.52% | -23.82% | **ETH** |

**Key observation:** ETH is outperforming BTC on very short-term (1–14 day) windows but underperforming on medium-term (30–200 day) windows. This divergence can signal an early-stage trend reversal in relative strength.

## 3. Bitcoin Dominance (BTC.D)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **BTC Dominance** | **56.49%** |
| **ETH Dominance** | **9.60%** |
| Total Crypto Market Cap | $2.503 trillion |
| BTC Market Cap | $1.411 trillion |
| ETH Market Cap | $0.240 trillion |

BTC dominance at **56.5%** is elevated. ETH dominance **below 10%** is historically depressed — a significant compression of Ethereum's market share. Historical context suggests that BTC dominance above ~57-60% has preceded rotation into alts/ETH in prior cycles, though the structural shift from ETFs may extend the current dynamic.

**Distance from All-Time Highs:**
- BTC: -44.2% from $126,080 (Oct 6, 2025)
- ETH: -59.8% from $4,946 (Aug 24, 2025)

ETH has more ground to recover and carries higher beta to any broad crypto recovery.

## 4. Ethereum Spot ETF Flows — Detailed Breakdown

### May 2026 Weekly Summary

| Week | Net Flow | Avg Daily |
|------|----------|-----------|
| May 1–8 (6 days) | **+$171.5m** | +$28.6m |
| May 11–15 (5 days) | **-$255.2m** | -$51.0m |
| May 18–22 (5 days) | **-$216.0m** | -$43.2m |
| May 26–29 (4 days) | **-$241.6m** | -$60.4m |
| **MAY TOTAL (20 days)** | **-$541.3m** | **-$27.1m/day** |

- **14 consecutive outflow sessions** from May 11 through May 29
- Only 5 of 20 trading days were positive
- Largest single-day inflow: +$101.2m (May 1)
- Largest single-day outflow: -$130.6m (May 12)
- **June 1 opened with continued outflows:** -$44.5m

### Individual ETF Performance (May 2026)

| ETF | Issuer | May Flows | All-Time Cumulative |
|-----|--------|-----------|---------------------|
| **ETHA** | BlackRock | **-$418.7m** | $11,399.9m |
| **FETH** | Fidelity | **-$87.3m** | $2,157.4m |
| **ETHE** | Grayscale (legacy) | **-$49.3m** | -$5,304.7m (net outflow) |
| **ETH** | Grayscale Mini | **-$29.5m** | $1,904.7m |
| **ETHB** | BlackRock | **+$35.5m** | $530.6m |
| **ETHW** | Bitwise | +$5.6m | $385.7m |

**ETHA (BlackRock)** drove the vast majority of outflows despite being the cumulative leader at $11.4B. **ETHB** was the only fund with meaningful positive flows, suggesting some rotation between BlackRock's two ETH products.

### Cumulative All Ethereum ETFs Since Inception: **$11.36 billion** (as of June 1, 2026)

### ETH vs BTC ETF Flow Comparison — May 2026

| Metric | Bitcoin ETFs | Ethereum ETFs |
|--------|-------------|--------------|
| May 2026 Net | **-$2,406.0m** | **-$541.3m** |
| Jun 1 | -$483.8m | -$44.5m |
| Positive days (May) | 6 of 20 | 5 of 20 |
| Largest daily outflow | -$733.4m (May 27) | -$130.6m (May 12) |
| **Cumulative All-Time** | **$55.2B** | **$11.4B** |
| **ETH/BTC flow ratio** | — | **20.6%** |

The ETH/BTC flow ratio of ~20.6% remains well below Ethereum's market cap share (~17% of BTC currently, ~25-30% historically), suggesting ETH ETFs continue to attract proportionally less institutional demand.

## 5. Trend Summary & Key Factors

**Bearish factors for ETH relative strength:**
- 9-month downtrend in ETH/BTC from 0.042 to 0.028
- 14 consecutive ETH ETF outflow sessions in May
- ETH dominance below 10% — historically depressed
- ETH down 60% from ATH vs BTC down 44%

**Potentially bullish contrarian signals:**
- ETH/BTC at 0.028 sits in the **lower third** of its 1-year range (0.022–0.042)
- Short-term relative strength has flipped to favor ETH (1–14 day windows)
- The May 28 low of 0.0272 and +3.7% bounce may indicate capitulation-level selling
- Analysts characterize the sustained ETF outflows as a potential "contrarian indicator"
- BTC dominance at 56.5% is approaching levels that historically preceded rotation into alts

**Upcoming potential catalysts for ETH:**
- Potential SEC approval of ETH ETF staking yields
- Continued L2 ecosystem maturation (Base, Arbitrum, Optimism)
- Post-Pectra upgrade effects on staking UX and L2 efficiency
